claim: Doubling engineering throughput requires treating Claude Code like a new senior hire: onboard to codebase conventions, write reusable skills, connect to production systems
actionable: Invest in comprehensive onboarding of Claude Code to your codebase—document Rails conventions, architecture patterns, testing standards, security rules—as you would for a new engineer.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom

claim: Platform commitment (single tool like Claude Code) compounds better than multi-tool strategies; switching costs and context fragmentation outweigh model differences
actionable: Choose one primary agent platform and commit to optimizing for it across your entire team—don't distribute effort across Cursor, Copilot, Augment unless there are specific feature requirements.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom

claim: Small, high-quality, testable skills improve faster through continuous improvement loops using real incident data and backtesting than multi-agent orchestrators
actionable: Build a library of focused, durable skills; validate them against real incident data; iterate based on what actually happened in production, not hypothetical scenarios.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom

claim: Giving agents problems instead of tasks (not 'run this skill' but 'take a look at this incident') causes agents to automatically invoke relevant skills and stay in context
actionable: Frame agent requests as problems to solve rather than specific tasks—describe the situation and let the agent choose which skills apply, keeping more context accessible.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom

claim: Automatic code approval with SOC 2 compliance requires careful labeling, backtesting, and human confidence validation, not just 'Claude approves PRs'
actionable: To safely implement automatic approvals, use backtesting on historical PRs, label confidence thresholds based on human validation, shape PRs toward safe/simple patterns.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom

claim: Agents write better code than humans when well-defined; properly designed agents don't degrade system safety—they remove human-based risks
actionable: Trust agents that have been properly trained on your codebase—well-written agents outperform inconsistent human reviewers and reduce rather than increase risk.
source_title: How Building with AI Can Double the Throughput of Your Engineering Team — Brian Scanlan, Intercom
